website.verification_hint: Compare the live file with release.json on the website repository main branch. The file does not embed its own website commit.

authority_boundary: A log records what happened. A ZLAR receipt records what counted as authorized effect when action tried to become consequence.

claim_boundary.summary: Registry recognition evidence boundary while preserving private result verification evidence, issuer-status evidence reviewability, explicit v3.4.31 release-asset byte-binding evidence, verifier-owned nested binding summary, nested artifact tamper-refusal evidence, readiness observed-summary contracts, terminal-chain recognition-refusal group case-ID evidence, recognition-refusal group evidence, named receipt-refusal evidence, verifier-kit diagnostics boundary, and recognition-contract digest boundary.
claim_boundary.evidence_threshold: v3.4.35 release checks pass with registry recognition evidence boundary: v3.4.35 release-forward verifier packets include trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_evidence in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and a Trusted Issuer Registry Recognition Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, binding the supplied recognition JSON, malformed-registry JSON, and malformed-registry error hashes; recording registry_type=trusted-receipt-issuers-v1, registry_evidence_model=bundled-local-fixture, live_probing=false, verdict=RECOGNIZED, issuer_status=active, signature_valid=true, and malformed_registry_fail_closed_before_verdict=true; and preserving false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, sovereign-recognition, public-attestation, public-attribution, and non-operator-review claim flags. The previous v3.4.34 release made private result verification evidence first-class: v3.4.34 release-forward verifier packets include a Private Result Verification Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, preserving private-result verification verdict, recomputed evidence-dir hash status, artifact count, sample/non-operator boundary, private_non_operator_pass_validated=false for generated sample evidence, and false public-attestation/public-attribution/public-non-operator-review claim flags. This is bundled-local-fixture registry recognition reviewability over generated release-forward evidence. It does not prove live registry state, live issuer status, key custody, revocation truth, production trust registry, production downstream recognition, production authority, sovereign recognition, public external attestation, real non-operator review, or enterprise readiness. v3.4.33 continues to provide issuer-status evidence reviewability: release-forward verifier packets include issuer_status_evidence in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and an Issuer Status Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, preserving the local hermetic issuer-status proof, verifier-kit issuer fixture verdict, active-issuer boarding, retired/compromised/missing-status/unknown/missing-key issuer refusals, no-live boundary, and false live/custody/revocation/production-registry/downstream claim flags. v3.4.32 continues the release-asset readiness handoff without widening asset claims: release-forward verifier dry runs use live explicit-tag GitHub release-asset reads plus --require-public public-distribution validation only for the explicit asset-bearing v3.4.31 public ZLAR-AI/ZLAR verifier-kit target; later proof-hardening releases do not inherit a public asset claim unless that exact release publishes the required assets, and North Star readiness rebuilds public-distribution posture from raw release-asset, reproducibility, and local dist-file evidence before accepting a public-ready claim. v3.4.31 remains the uploaded byte-bound verifier-kit asset evidence: live public release assets for the verifier kit are published, zlar verifier-kit-release-assets-live-read emits zlar-verifier-kit-release-assets-live-v1 with all required assets present and downloaded, and zlar verifier-kit-public-distribution --require-public reports public_distribution_posture_ready with ready_for_public_distribution_claim=true. v3.4.30 proof-smoke, North Star readiness, and release-forward evidence preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_binding as a verifier-owned artifact-verification summary with nested artifact body hashes, verified flags, binding booleans, and false stronger-claim flags. v3.4.28+ terminal-chain reports and portable artifacts embed nested generated preflight and service-proof artifacts, and release-forward dry-run exports terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals with forged_inner_preflight_hash_refused=true and forged_inner_service_hash_refused=true after refusing forged inner preflight and service-proof hashes while recomputing outer terminal-chain artifact integrity. Prior readiness evidence remains preserved: v3.4.25+ proof-smoke and North Star readiness preserve group_count=3, case_count=18, and exact grouped case IDs for both terminal-chain evidence and terminal-chain artifact verification. For v3.4.26+ the Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries mirror that same case-ID contract. v3.4.24+ release-forward verifier packets still preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_required=true, and all_recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_preserved=true for no usable recognized receipt authority, recognized receipt scope mismatch, and route/request authority material refusal groups. v3.4.23+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve recognition_refusal_groups_sha256 and grouped before-mutation booleans. v3.4.22+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve named_receipt_refusals_sha256 and named refusal booleans for missing, invalid, stale, unknown-issuer, wrong-policy, wrong-domain, and wrong-tool receipt cases. v3.4.21+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve zlar-verifier-kit-external-runner-diagnostics-v1.json, and v3.4.19+ readiness, proof-smoke, and release-forward dry-run checks still preserve installed-runtime-profile recognition-contract SHA-256 binding.
claim_boundary.safe_codex_wording: ZLAR can govern Codex CLI-invoked MCP tool calls when those MCP servers are routed through ZLAR.
non_claims: No hook, user, or machine configuration claim.
